title: test_unicode_sanitizer fails on emoji sequences
description: The test test_unicode_sanitizer in sanitizer/tests/test_unicode_sanitizer.py fails when processing emoji sequences that contain zero-width joiners (ZWJ) and variation selectors. The expected output is a clean plain-text representation, but the sanitizer currently leaves some ZWJ characters and does not normalize skin-tone modifiers consistently. Example input: 👨‍👩‍👧, 👩🏽‍💻. The bug report indicates the issue is related to the unicode.sanitize module's handling of combining marks and zero-width joiners. Our previous investigation (NOTE-202) suspected that the character look-up table is missing entries for ZWJ and variation selectors within emoji sequences.

title: Spike: replace sanitizer with third-party unicodedata2
description: Old research spike evaluating the unicodedata2 library for the sanitizer. Superseded by tracking issue BUG-421; retained only for historical reference.

title: Add ZWJ emoji cases to sanitizer test suite
description: Follow-up task to extend the sanitizer test suite with more ZWJ emoji sequences once the fix lands.
